name: inviteflood
title: inviteflood

commands.name: inviteflood

arguments.metavar: IPv4 source IP address default is IP address of interface
arguments.raw: -i IPv4 source IP address [default is IP address of interface]

arguments.metavar: destPort 0 - 65535 default is well-known SIP port 5060
lineString line used by SNOM default is blank
arguments.raw: -l lineString line used by SNOM [default is blank]

arguments.metavar: sleep time btwn INVITE msgs usec

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# inviteflood -h
 
 inviteflood - Version 2.0
             June 09, 2006
Usage:
Mandatory -
 	interface (e.g. eth0)
 	target user (e.g. "" or john.doe or 5000 or "1+210-555-1212")
 	target domain (e.g. enterprise.com or an IPv4 address)
 	IPv4 addr of flood target (ddd.ddd.ddd.ddd)
 	flood stage (i.e. number of packets)
Optional -
 	-a flood tool "From:" alias (e.g. jane.doe)
 	-i IPv4 source IP address [default is IP address of interface]
 	-S srcPort  (0 - 65535) [default is well-known discard port 9]
 	-D destPort (0 - 65535) [default is well-known SIP port 5060]
 	-l lineString line used by SNOM [default is blank]
 	-s sleep time btwn INVITE msgs (usec)
 	-h help - print this usage
 	-v verbose output mode

examples.command:
inviteflood eth0 5000 example.local 192.168.1.5 100

inviteflood - Version 2.0
              June 09, 2006

source IPv4 addr:port   = 192.168.1.202:9
dest   IPv4 addr:port   = 192.168.1.5:5060
targeted UA             = 5000@192.168.1.1

Flooding destination with 100 packets
sent: 100

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=2c6b3fd019be5669478bfdabeeac5cf25ea259d1, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
SIP/SDP INVITE message flooding over UDP/IP
A tool to perform SIP/SDP INVITE message flooding over
UDP/IP. It was tested on a Linux Red Hat Fedora Core 4
platform (Pentium IV, 2.5 GHz), but it is expected this
tool will successfully build and execute on a variety of
Linux distributions.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use inviteflood because ### inviteflood.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair inviteflood with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `inviteflood --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### inviteflood.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install inviteflood
Install with: sudo apt install inviteflood
